message:
refactor(fp-frame): remove dead DuplicateIndexUnsupported variant (br-yvkrb / fd90.145)

fd90.145: follow-on to fd90.140's docs cleanup. The variant
FrameError::DuplicateIndexUnsupported was declared at line 113 but
never constructed anywhere in the workspace:

  grep -rnE 'DuplicateIndexUnsupported' crates/
  → only the declaration; zero call-site constructions

The error message ('duplicate index labels are unsupported in strict
mode for MVP slice') hints it was reserved for future strict-mode
work that hasn't materialized in 1+ years.

The enum is #[non_exhaustive] (line 108), so removing a variant is
SemVer-safe — downstream code can't construct or exhaustively match
the enum without acknowledging future additions/removals. If strict-
mode duplicate handling lands later, the variant can be re-added
with real construction sites (and the README's Error Architecture
row will pick up the change at that point).

Verified:
- cargo build -p fp-frame compiles
- cargo test -p fp-frame --lib — 1434 passed; 0 failed

Pure dead-code removal. No behavior change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -109,8 +109,6 @@ use unicode_casefold::UnicodeCaseFold;
 pub enum FrameError {
     #[error("index length ({index_len}) does not match column length ({column_len})")]
     LengthMismatch { index_len: usize, column_len: usize },
-    #[error("duplicate index labels are unsupported in strict mode for MVP slice")]
-    DuplicateIndexUnsupported,
     #[error("compatibility gate rejected operation: {0}")]
     CompatibilityRejected(String),
     #[error(transparent)]
